## Step 2: Make Prediction - Runtime Error

Note: Since `nextGeneration` already exists and is exported, we skip directly to the runtime error stage (no compilation error possible).

```
Red Phase - Runtime Error Prediction:
- Test: "should bring a dead cell to life when it has exactly three living neighbors (reproduction)"
- Input: [[0, 0], [2, 0], [1, 1]] - dead cell [1, 0] has 3 living neighbors
- Expected: Runtime assertion error
- Expected value: result contains [1, 0]
- Actual value: result will NOT contain [1, 0] (current implementation only filters living cells, never births dead ones)
- Reason: Current implementation only considers cells already alive; it filters them by survival rules. There is no logic to bring dead cells to life.
- Diff:
  expected [...] to deep equally contain [ 1, 0 ]
  Received: [] (all 3 cells die from underpopulation: [0,0] has 1 neighbor, [2,0] has 1 neighbor, [1,1] has 2 neighbors so it survives)
```

## Step 3: Run Test - Verify Runtime Error

content.text:
Test fails as predicted with a runtime assertion error. The result `[[1, 1]]` does not contain `[1, 0]`. My prediction was slightly off on the exact contents of the returned array (I predicted `[]`, but actually [1,1] survives because it has 2 neighbors), but the core prediction was correct: the dead cell [1, 0] is not brought to life.

Let me note: my detailed counting was wrong. With [[0,0], [2,0], [1,1]]:
- [0,0]: neighbors are [2,0]? No (distance 2 in x). [1,1]? Yes. → 1 neighbor → dies
- [2,0]: neighbors are [0,0]? No. [1,1]? Yes. → 1 neighbor → dies
- [1,1]: neighbors are [0,0]? Yes. [2,0]? Yes. → 2 neighbors → survives

So result is `[[1, 1]]`, which matches the actual output. The key prediction (test fails because [1,0] not in result) was correct.

## Red Phase Complete

```
Red Phase Complete:
Test Activated: "should bring a dead cell to life when it has exactly three living neighbors (reproduction)"
Prediction: Runtime assertion error - [1, 0] not in result. Correct
Result: AssertionError: expected [ [ 1, 1 ] ] to deep equally contain [ 1, 0 ]

Test state: 6 passed | 1 failed | 1 todo

Proceeding to Green phase.
```
